name: Valle del Río Galera por Orce — circular desde Huéscar
region_intro: Road cycling around Huéscar, located in the Granada Geopark region of Andalusia, offers diverse landscapes for cyclists. The area features prominent mountain ranges like the Sierra de La Sagra, providing opportunities for challenging climbs and scenic vistas. Cyclists can also find routes around reservoirs such as Embalse de San Clemente and Embalse de Portillo, as well as along river valleys like the Río Guardal. The terrain includes mostly paved surfaces, suitable for road bikes, with varied elevation profiles. Best road cycling routes around Huéscar The most popular road cycling route is Embalse del Anchuricas by the Mirador de La Novia — circular by the Sierra de la Graña, a 35.5 miles (57.1 km) trail that takes 3 hours 15 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain through mountainous terrain. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Valle del Río Galera por Orce — circular desde Huéscar, a moderate 30.4 miles (49.0 km) path. This route explores the valley of the Río Galera, offering varied scenery. Local road cyclists also love the Huéscar Historic Center – Huéscar loop from Huéscar, a 33.9 miles (54.5 km) trail leading through the historic center and surrounding areas, often completed in about 2 hours 27 minutes. Road cycling around Huéscar is defined by mountain ranges like the Sierra de La Sagra, scenic reservoirs, and river valleys within the Granada Geopark. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to challenging excursions. The routes in Huéscar are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars. More than 300 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Huéscar's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain and difficulty can I expect for road cycling in Huéscar?
A: Road cycling around Huéscar offers a diverse range of terrain. You'll find routes with challenging climbs through prominent mountain ranges like the Sierra de La Sagra, as well as more moderate rides along river valleys and around reservoirs. The area features mostly paved surfaces suitable for road bikes. Out of 30 available routes, 12 are considered difficult, 16 are moderate, and 2 are easy, catering to various fitness levels.

Q: Are there road cycling routes that explore the Sierra de La Sagra?
A: Yes, the Sierra de La Sagra is a prominent feature in the region, offering opportunities for challenging rides and impressive vistas. A notable route that explores this area is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1277356362/embalse-del-anchuricas-by-the-mirador-de-la-novia-circular-by-the-sierra-de-la-grana" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse del Anchuricas by the Mirador de La Novia — circular by the Sierra de la Graña&lt;/a&gt;, which is a difficult 35.5-mile trail with significant elevation gain.

Q: Can I find road cycling routes that pass by reservoirs or rivers?
A: Absolutely. The region around Huéscar features scenic routes around bodies of water. You can cycle around the Embalse de San Clemente or the Embalse de Portillo. Routes also follow river valleys like the Río Guardal, which has historical monuments such as the 16th-century Puente de las Ánimas along its course. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1350385332/valle-del-rio-galera-por-orce-circular-desde-huescar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valle del Río Galera por Orce — circular desde Huéscar&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate route that explores a river valley.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while road cycling around Huéscar?
A: Beyond the stunning mountain landscapes of the Sierra de La Sagra and serene reservoirs, you'll be cycling within the unique Granada Geopark, known for its geological formations. You might also encounter the century-old giant sequoias. For specific natural attractions, consider visiting the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5297752" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castril Gorge&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6226243" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vega of the Orce River&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, many road cycling routes around Huéscar are designed as circular loops. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20123600" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Huéscar Historic Center – Huéscar loop from Huéscar&lt;/a&gt; is a popular 33.9-mile circular trail. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20123511" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roadbike loop from Huéscar&lt;/a&gt;, covering about 25 miles.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy the most about road cycling in Huéscar?
A: The road cycling routes in Huéscar are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars. Cyclists often praise the diverse landscapes, from challenging mountain climbs to scenic rides around reservoirs, and the generally quiet, well-paved roads that make for an excellent cycling experience.

Q: Are there any challenging road cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Yes, Huéscar offers several challenging routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20123692" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castril Village – Portillo Reservoir loop from Fátima&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 52.7-mile route with significant elevation gain, perfect for experienced cyclists looking for a demanding ride through mountainous terrain.

Q: What historical or cultural sites can I see along the road cycling routes?
A: While cycling, you can explore the historic town of Huéscar itself, which features manor houses and religious buildings like the 16th-century Colegiata de Santa María. The Río Guardal also has historical monuments along its course, such as the Puente de las Ánimas. The broader Andalusian region offers a rich cultural experience with traditional bars and local cuisine.

Q: Are there any shelters or huts available near the cycling routes?
A: Yes, if you're exploring the more remote areas, there are several shelters and huts. You can find places like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3980308" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Campo del Espino Shelter&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6002950" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Refugio Cañada Humosa&lt;/a&gt;, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6373386" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Banderillas Shelter&lt;/a&gt;, which can be useful for longer excursions.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Huéscar?
A: The region's climate makes spring and autumn ideal for road cycling, offering pleasant temperatures. Summers can be hot, especially in the middle of the day, while winters are generally mild but can have cooler temperatures at higher elevations. Checking the local weather forecast before your ride is always recommended.

Q: Are there options for shorter, moderate road cycling routes?
A: Yes, there are plenty of moderate options. Many routes are designed to be completed within a few hours, offering varied scenery without extreme difficulty. A good example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1350385332/valle-del-rio-galera-por-orce-circular-desde-huescar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valle del Río Galera por Orce — circular desde Huéscar&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 30.4-mile path exploring the Río Galera valley.
tips: The Río Galera that goes from Castillejar to the town that bears the same name, Galera, we encounter this area of fluvial terraces that the river itself has created and that leaves us with a beautiful valley, comparing the greenest areas of the valley floor with the silt lands with low plants.
Road that approaches the center of Galera from where you drive through a set of hills and agricultural fields without tree vegetation and gray land.

Orce is one of the most beautiful towns to visit in the north of the province of Granada. It is characterized by its constructions, such as the Parish of Santa María and the well-known Alcazaba de las Siete Torres, with the famous seven pillars.

waypoints.name: Alcazaba of the Seven Towers of Orce
